draft.description:
The spec is being written or actively revised. Not yet submitted for
acceptance review. Free to change in shape, scope, and content.

withdraw.description:
Withdrawal from draft preserves the document as a "considered, not
pursued" record without going through review.

under_review.description:
The spec has been submitted for acceptance review. Content is frozen
for the duration of the review; comments are tracked, but edits during
review require returning to draft.

accepted.description:
The spec has passed review and represents intended behavior or
capability. Implementation work may begin. The status corresponds to
"Status | Accepted" in the spec document header.

complete.description:
The spec has been fully implemented and verified against its completion
verification gate. Corresponds to "Status | Complete" in current POM
specs. Terminal, but admits the supersede out-transition explicitly.

superseded.description:
A later spec replaces this one. The current spec is retained in repo
for history but no longer authoritative. Terminal.

withdrawn.description:
The author retracts the spec before acceptance. The spec is preserved
in repo as a "considered, not pursued" record. Terminal.

rejected.description:
The spec is refused on review. Preserved as a documented rejection.
Terminal.

pom.description:
POM SPEC document lifecycle. A SPEC starts as a draft, may be reviewed and
accepted, may be implemented to completion, may be superseded by a later
SPEC, may be withdrawn before acceptance, or may be rejected on review.
Pause states (Deferred/Blocked/Waiting) are deliberately not modeled as
primary states in this version.

events.name: submit_for_review
events.description: Author declares the draft ready for acceptance review.

events.name: request_changes
events.description: Reviewer asks for changes; spec returns to draft.

events.name: implement_and_verify
events.description:
Implementation work is complete and the spec's verification gate
(Goal-Backward Check + Scenario Tests + Semantic Validation, where
applicable) is satisfied.

events.name: supersede
events.description:
A new spec is published that replaces this one. Triggered by the
acceptance of the superseding spec.

events.name: withdraw
events.description: The author retracts the spec before acceptance.

guards.name: has_completion_verification_gate
guards.description:
The spec declares an explicit completion verification gate. Required
to move from accepted to complete. The gate combines Goal-Backward
Check, Scenario Tests, and Semantic Validation as defined in
CONTEXT.md.

guards.name: superseding_spec_accepted
guards.description:
The spec that supersedes this one is itself in state accepted or
complete. Prevents supersession by drafts.

invariants:
A spec in state complete cannot return to any non-terminal state.
Updates to a completed spec require a new spec that supersedes it.

Withdrawal is only possible from draft. After review starts, the
outcome must be acceptance, rejection, or further revision; the
author cannot quietly retract.

Rejection is terminal. A rejected spec is not reopened; a fresh
spec must be drafted instead. (This is a model choice to be
validated; see open points in EXPERIMENT.md.)

A spec cannot be superseded by another spec that is itself in
draft or under_review. The superseding_spec_accepted guard
enforces this at the workflow level.
open_points:
Pause states (Deferred, Blocked, Waiting from prompts/15) are not
modeled as primary states. If real usage shows the need, options are:
(a) add them as primary states (paying the combinatorial cost),
(b) treat them as an orthogonal modifier on the entity,
(c) reserve them to PROJECT_STATE and not to the spec lifecycle.
Rejection is modeled as terminal. POM may want to allow a "revise
and resubmit" path that does not require a brand-new spec id.
The accepted -> withdrawn path is intentionally absent. If real
practice shows accepted specs being retracted (not superseded), the
model needs adjustment.
Under-review changes return to draft. An alternative is a
"revisions_requested" state that keeps the review thread open.
closed_points:
"complete admits supersede out-transition" — resolved via the
schema attribute re_entry_allowed: true. The validator no longer
flags W003 on complete. Same decision applied to ticket-lifecycle's
"closed" state.
